--- title: "xxx" author: "Aiden W. Harig" date: "2026-02-16" execute: warning: false echo: false categories: [xxx] --- ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: # Preface What drives national wellbeing – wealth, longevity, or both? Wealthier nations tend to report higher levels of happiness, but economic growth may not increase wellbeing at a constant rate. At the same time, longer life expectancy reflects improvements in health, stability, and quality of life, which may also contribute to how people evaluate their lives. In this analysis, I examine how wellbeing relates to **GDP per capita** and **Life Expectancy**, first individually and then jointly. The goal is to determine whether wealth exhibits diminishing returns and whether economic prosperity or longevity plays the stronger independent role in predicting national wellbeing.

# **Modeling Wellbeing as a Function of GDP per Capita**
